title: backdrop-filter
description: Applies graphical effects — blur, brightness, saturation — to the area rendered behind an element. The classic use is frosted glass: a translucent panel that blurs what's underneath. Baseline newly available since 2022.
oldWay:
/* No CSS equivalent existed.
   Hacks: duplicate the background, offset it,
   and clip it to the card area. Required JavaScript
   to keep in sync on scroll. Still imperfect. */
html:
Glass Card

backdrop-filter blurs what is behind this panel.
challenge: The card sits over the colored blobs but looks plain — you can see they're behind it but nothing is blurred. Add backdrop-filter: blur(12px) to .glass-card to create a frosted glass effect.

alternatives.name: Combine with brightness or saturation
alternatives.description: backdrop-filter accepts multiple functions. Adding saturate() makes the blur more vivid — a common refinement for dark glass panels.

realWorldUsage: Navigation bars that remain legible while a page scrolls behind them, floating tooltips over complex UI, modal dialogs over a blurred page, and sticky sidebars with a frosted panel effect — any UI layer that needs to feel separated from the content behind it without an opaque background.
